description: Comparative analysis of such issues as state formation, state-society relations, the politics of economic development, tensions between authoritarianism and democracy, and the politics of identity, with particular attention to a select number of major countries of Southeast Asia. Not open to students with credit for POLI SCI 639 prior to fall 2017

prerequisites.prerequisites_text: Sophomore standing and (POLI SCI 120or182) or (POLI SCI 106 or 186 taken prior to fall 2017) or graduate standing
